generated_assessment.reasoning: The T4 orchestrated counter-argument adaptation (with corroborating threat-salience and elite-distrust cues operating over weeks) targets evaluative attitudes. This 40-year-old female has very low agreeableness (mean 14th pct) — especially compliance (4.9th pct) and trust (24.1st pct) — and low tender-mindedness (22.1st pct), making her highly resistant to sympathetic appeals but also structurally receptive to adversarial, tough-minded pieces of evidence that attack institutional credibility. Her very moderate neuroticism (27th pct) and low anxiety (38.9th pct) mean the threat-salience framing lands but does not produce panic-driven avoidance; she tolerates the cognitive pressure of multi-turn argumentation. Her moderately open values (51.9th pct) and low deliberation (1.1th pct) make her willing to update position if the arguments appear novel and forceful, but her low compliance and strong skepticism prevent major swings despite ALIGNED direction. The result is a clear but contained intensification, moving from -273 to -331 (58 points).

attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts
